card.intuition: A set of three specific metrics that Google uses to measure the user experience of a webpage: loading speed, interactivity, and visual stability.
card.analogy: A health checkup for a website: the doctor checks your heart rate (Loading), your reaction time (Interactivity), and your balance (Stability). If one is bad, you're not fully 'healthy'.
when_to_use: Improving SEO rankings (Google uses these as ranking signals)
Identifying specific bottlenecks in page loading
gotchas: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) is often delayed by hero images that aren't optimized
CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) is usually caused by images or ads loading without pre-defined height/width, causing content to 'jump'

steps.label: Navigation
steps.annotation: Browser starts loading — performance timeline begins.

steps.label: LCP
steps.annotation: Largest Contentful Paint: time until largest image/text visible. Target < 2.5s.

steps.label: FID / INP
steps.annotation: Interaction to Next Paint: latency of first user interaction. Target < 200ms.

steps.label: CLS
steps.annotation: Cumulative Layout Shift: unexpected visual shifts. Target < 0.1.

steps.label: Score
steps.annotation: Vitals aggregated into performance score. Affects SEO ranking.

exercise.prompt: Identify the three main Core Web Vital metrics by their acronyms.
exercise.starter_code:
vitals = [
    "1. ___ (Largest Contentful Paint) - Loading",
    "2. ___ (Cumulative Layout Shift) - Stability",
    "3. ___ (Interaction to Next Paint) - Interactivity"
]
exercise.test_code:
assert "LCP" in vitals[0]
assert "CLS" in vitals[1]
assert "INP" in vitals[2]
print("PASS")
